entry.title: Re: Production order settlement
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In addition to above can you suggest how we can know if the order has been settled or not. And the closed status does we have to manually closed the order or it will automatically get closed after settlement has been done.

Dear all,

Find the attachment for same.

Nikunj

entry.title: Process order settlement
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to know how we can find out whether order has been settled or not. I have tried KOB1 t code but where to check in it that ensure order has been settled. Also, can you suggest that the order closed status get automatically set after settlement or we have to manually do that.

Appreciate any help on this.

Regards,

Shubham

entry.title: Re: Adobe form with RAP Based OData Service
entry.content: Hi @DiegoValdivia, Thanks for reading the blog. I completely agree with you. SAP suggests extension of data provider through Custom Fields & Logic app, but very limited fragmented forms have BADIs to achieve that.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to have the In-build translation featu...
entry.content:
Hi @Anna, 

@DirkManuel is right. If you export the entire package in the SCORM format, the Machine Translation will not work, as it's provided through the SAP Enable Now Manager.

However, there is one option (undocumented, of course; otherwise, it wouldn't be interesting) to make it work. We'll use the same trick SAP Enable Now utilizes to export content directly to SAP SuccessFactors LMS, but it should work with any LMS 🚀  .

So, export the course as a SCORM package. Then extract from the SCORM .zip file a single file called imsmanifest.xml

Open it with Notepad or Notepad++ and replace all occurrences of "index.html?" with the full published path to the content in your SAP Enable Now system. 

Example: Before/After

BeforeAfter

Then, archive the modified imsmanifest.xml into a separate .zip archive.

Upload this .zip archive as a SCORM package to your LMS.

The LMS should accept it. So, the course seems to be "published" in your LMS; however, the content will be played from your SAP Enable Now system, and the Machine Translation function will work. Also, you can keep updating your course in SAP Enable Now without re-publishing it to LMS.

See, I could run it in the SCORM Cloud, and the Machine Translation works. 😍

However, it has a limitation: the content tracking won't work. 😫 This limitation also applies to the "direct SCORM export to SAP SuccessFactors."

entry.title: Re: SAP PROJECT SYSTEM (PS)
entry.content:
Hello Arpit,

SAP PS is part of the S/4 HANA core. So you don't need to worry about its future usability.  As you are starting fresh on SAP PS, It is best to keep looking at What's New information from SAP and Regularly checking SAP Help Portal for SAP Project System to gain knowledge into PS functionality. 

Also make sure you understand the differences between SAP PS in Public Cloud and SAP PS in private Cloud/On- Premise.

Best Regards

Sammar

entry.title: Re: Basis - Bulk user query based on T-Code
entry.content:
Hi 

Use the transaction code SUIM or S_BCE_68001398 to find the list of users having access to the specified transaction code.

Regards

SS

entry.title: VALID_FROM error using CSAP_MAT_BOM_CREATE
entry.content:
I can run CSAP_MAT_BOM_CREATE successfully, but after only adding VALID_FROM with any date such as "2024-01-01", it will run with exception.

Please kindly guide how to solve it. Many thanks!

is there any plan to have the "Advanced intercompany stock transfer order" available in a 2-Tier Scenario ? means Supplying Company is on private cloud and receiving/selling company in a public cloud edition ? 

 

Thanks

Daniel

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi @Suzanne_Heiob, what will be the difference of the new assessment and old assessment?

For example, old assessment focuses on the information from new release, is it the same for new assessment but covers past 2-3 releases?

What is the passing rate of new assessment? Old assessment requires 100% passing rating if no. of question below a threshold. How about the new assessment?

Thanks,

Carson

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Thanks for publishing this , but Why are we trying to make simple things complex? When there are so many useful things that SAP can do with SAP CI which are in queue since more than 2 years why are we working on things like this. 

SAP PO was very rigid and SAP CI simplified things to a great deal. This particular pipeline not only makes things more complex and rigid than SAP PO but also takes away all the flexibility. This will leave a consultant puzzled like they were while learning SAP PO. Of all the integration flows I have worked on  Interface determination are not needed for even 0.1 % of the scenarios. The problems this design claims to solve were already solved! This just packages those solution into a more rigid and complex setup.

  • Ensures decoupling and Exactly Once processing for asynchronous processing, also for split scenarios and in case of restart - JMS queue already ensures this this is nothing new
  • Provides sophisticated restart capabilities, i.e., automatic retries as well as manual retry options - Every JMS queue inherently provides restart capability I am afraid I cannot find the sophistication
  • Simplifies operations by separating errors into different generic queues separated by routing, mapping and receiver system delivery - Generic Queues? the default 30 queues that we get have a limit of 10GB if a customer choses to go live with these pipelines and route 90% of their integrations like (400-1000) through 8 queues and each queue has a max size of 300mb upto 90% of total queue size (9GB) than that will never work! Messages will overflow and will get lost. 
  • Requires lower number of JMS queues to take into account the resource limits of an SAP Integration Suite tenant which at the end also simplifies operations - We can use just 2 queues as well by writing more complex logic than this but using lesser number of queues without increasing queue size doesnt solve anything
  • Allows isolation and tuning of parallelization for individual receiver systems - this works in individual queues as well
  • Allows reusability of artifacts across multiple flows by using generic flow concept - Process direct has always existed and enabled generic flows
  • Simplifies integration flow model especially for content based router and recipient list scenarios - again increased complexity
  • Simplifies configuration on the backend sender side, e.g., just one ALE port on SAP S/4HANA system required - this was always an option by having a single port in we21 and then routing it to multiple iflows in SAP CI nothing new here

    SAP should address the low number of queues in Integration suite. If a customer is paying 6000$ for a license and then paying additional fee for the additional bandwidth which again goes in thousands SAP should remove a limit on default 30 queues. With memory being so cheap now a days restricting a tenant to 32 GB of logging space and 10GB of queues doesn't really cut it.

entry.title: Re: Back To Standard - S/4HANA Upgrade Technical Brownfie...
entry.content:
Hi,

there are a lot of open SAP courses for both topics, like:

Key Functional Topics in a System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA | openSAP

Guide Your SAP S/4HANA Project to Success | openSAP

 

Kind regards

Robert

entry.title: Re: Date Picker is Blank (IOS)
entry.content:

Any update on this. need this asap. thanks you!

entry.title: Failing to import ABAP Flight Reference Scenario in S/4 HANA 2023 system
entry.content:
I have tried, very unsuccessfully, to import the ABAP Flight Reference Scenario to our on-prem S/4 HANA 2023 system.

I tried twice importing the 2023 branch from SAP-samples/abap-platform-refscen-flight at ABAP-platform-2023 (github.com), using a downloaded zip file as our system is blocked by a firewall from connecting directly to github.com

I have documented my attempt and all the errors extensively here:

Failure to import 2023 Branch · Issue #36 · SAP-samples/abap-platform-refscen-flight (github.com)

Has anyone had a similar experience and perhaps managed to do this successfully?

Unfortunately, there is a disclaimer on the repo that there is no support for the Flight Reference Model.

However, the SAP Help suggests to customers to use this repo and abapGit to import it:

Downloading the ABAP Flight Reference Scenario | SAP Help Portal

(It is furthermore baffling to me how much SAP promotes and endorses abapGit when that is developed completely independently and without any support from SAP!).

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Product Release Webcast - May 2024
entry.content: Advisor

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am Ajay new to join SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: Calling BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMIT from PO
entry.content:
Hello,

as you have mentioned S/4, you should consider to not use BAPIs anymore, as those are not "clean core" - use instead the released API:

https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_SALES_ORDER_SRV_0001

It should be available from Release 2020 FP2

There is a risk that a BAPI becomes deprecated or is not maintained with new features for new releases at a certain point in time.

If you want to stick on the BAPI, there is a way to generate an IDOC umbrella - i never did it myself, but just asked google and there a tons of tutorials round that. Then you deliver asynchronously from PI the data via IDOC to SAP and with this facade it's calling the BAPI - so you don't need to rework your logic - only rename the segments. A good example is SAP standard way on creating Purchase orders, where the IDOC interface is a rendered BAPI. 

Hope that helps you.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: How to have manually created hardcoded rows/columns translated in SAP SAC
entry.content: same issue here, have you solve it?

entry.title: Re: Custom Analytical Queries / Webdynpro: Characteristic...

Hello,

we implemented 3398557 which resulted in the query jumping into debugger. After implementing SAP Note 3385969 it's working just fine now.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Cross Landscape Distribution - Focused Build Standalone Extensions Part 1

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI Sunset
entry.content:
Hi, 

"Even after moving everything to Fiori" - that's wrong. We have a lot of transactions, business processes for which no Fiori-possibility exist, so we need GUI in future.

"we still have access to SAP GUI for users who do not wish to move to Fiori" because the performance of Fiori is slower than the same process in GUI.

Best regards

Burkhard

entry.title: Re: Long-Term Planning functionality (LTP) in S/4HANA Clo...
entry.content:
Hello @SAPSupport 

That is a really bold statement to put pMRP and long term planning and very misleading.

pMRP lacks several functionalities that long term planning provides:

  • purchasing budging - with long term planning you can copy the planning results of raw (purchased) materials to Purchasing Information System - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/a0d3efbac8b14fc89b29bf47a1677c86/c173b6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4.html Purchasing Information System allows not only for the planning results analysis, but it also gathers actual data, so you can compare plan vs actual.
  • stock levels planning - long term planning results can be copied to Inventory Control https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/a0d3efbac8b14fc89b29bf47a1677c86/c473b6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4.html Similarly to PURCHIS, Inventory Control lets you analyze the planning results, but it also gathers actual inventory data, providing for plan vs actual tracking
  • integration with CO - long term planning results can be transferred to CO as activity type requirements https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/a0d3efbac8b14fc89b29bf47a1677c86/c773b6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4.html That is essential to know how much effort (man hours, machine hours) you need to realize your long term planning. Once you know activity type requirements you can calculate activity rates, that are essential to product cost calculation

Those features are not there in pMRP - can you export pMRP planning results of various scenarios to a S/4HANA standard planning tool and make plan vs actual comparisons there? As far as I know, pMRP is simply not integrated with purchasing planning, stock planning or Controlling. 

My understanding is that pMRP is more of a operational level planning tool, not master planning one. The way I see it S/4HANA lacks descent long term/master planning tools as those functions are transferred to IBP.

One more point, long term planning is included in the basic license. Is pMRP also comprised in the basic S/4HANA license or is it chargable separately?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Story will not load
entry.content:
Hello @NPH,

We had a similar issue this week. We were able to solve it by opening the story on a different page by adding parameter "&page=3" to the URL. We had only one working page in our story so it could be that you try out a bit. Once the story opened in edit mode we were able to duplicate the non working pages and delete the original pages afterwards. Maybe it's worth a try if this procedure works on your story, too.

Best regards

Lothar

entry.title: Re: Alternative to Fiori and oData, Vue and Rest Api
entry.content:
Here is another example of Rest Call usage in Fiori.

In this example, we are making a rest get call to Binance to get current ticker price.

To learn how to create a Fiori SAP UI5 app in VSCode, you can read that blog post.

To avoid CORS errors, install cors-anywhere

 

npm i cors-anywhere

 

To run multiple scripts, install npm run all

 

npm install -g npm-run-all

 

Controller Code

 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
],
    /**
     *  {typeof sap.ui.core.mvc.Controller} Controller
     */
    function (Controller,JSONModel,MsgBox) {
        "use strict";

        return Controller.extend("com.blogspot.javafish.fiorirestapp.controller.ViewMain", {
            onInit: function () {
                this.fetchData("BTCUSDT");                
            },
            fetchData:function(symbol){
                var that = this;
                $.ajax({
                    type : "GET",
                    url : "http://localhost:8081/https://api.binance.com/api/v3/ticker/price?symbol=" + symbol,
                    contentType : "application/json",
                    dataType : "json",
                    success:function(respData) {
                        that.setViewData(respData);
                    }                    
                }).always(function(data,status,response){
                    //MsgBox.show("Data:" + data  + " Status:" + status + " Response:" + response); 
                });
            },
            getPriceForSymbol:function(){
                var modelData = this.getView().getModel().getData();
                let sym = "" + modelData.payload.symbol;
                sym = sym.toUpperCase(); 
                this.fetchData(sym);
            },
            setViewData:function(data){
                var oModel = new JSONModel();
                oModel.setData({
                    "payload":data 
                });
                this.getView().setModel(oModel);
            }
        });
    });

 

View File Code

 

<mvc:View xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns:form="sap.ui.layout.form" controllerName="com.blogspot.javafish.fiorirestapp.controller.ViewMain"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" displayBlock="true"
    xmlns="sap.m">
    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <content>
        <form:SimpleForm id="_IDGenSimpleForm1" xmlns:sap.ui.layout.form="sap.ui.layout.form">
        <form:content>
            <core:Title text="Sample Form" id="lblHead"/>
            <Label text="Symbol" id="lblSymbol"></Label>
            <Input width="200px" id="txtSymbol" value="{/payload/symbol}"></Input>
            <Label text="Price" id="lblPrice"></Label>
            <Input width="200px" id="txtPrice" value="{/payload/price}"></Input>
        </form:content>
        </form:SimpleForm>
        <Button text="Get Price" icon="sap-icon://save" press="getPriceForSymbol" id="btnGetPrice"></Button>
        </content>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

Cors-anywhere Proxy settings

 

// Listen on a specific host via the HOST environment variable
var host = process.env.HOST || '0.0.0.0';
// Listen on a specific port via the PORT environment variable
var port = process.env.PORT || 8081;
 
var cors_proxy = require('cors-anywhere');
cors_proxy.createServer({
    originWhitelist: [], // Allow all origins    
    requireHeader: ['Origin', '*'],
    removeHeaders: ['cookie', 'cookie2']
}).listen(port, host, function() {
    console.log('Running CORS Anywhere on ' + host + ':' + port);
});

 

Package Json

 

{
  "name": "fiorirestapp",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "private": true,
  "description": "A Fiori application.",
  "keywords": [
    "ui5",
    "openui5",
    "sapui5"
  ],
  "main": "webapp/index.html",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling": "1",
    "@ui5/cli": "^3.10.1",
    "cors-anywhere": "^0.4.3"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "fiori run --open \"test/flpSandbox.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#comblogspotjavafishfiorirestapp-display\"",
    "start-local": "fiori run --config ./ui5-local.yaml --open \"test/flpSandbox.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#comblogspotjavafishfiorirestapp-display\"",
    "build": "ui5 build --config=ui5.yaml --clean-dest --dest dist",
    "deploy": "fiori verify",
    "deploy-config": "fiori add deploy-config",
    "start-noflp": "fiori run --open \"index.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false\"",
    "start-variants-management": "fiori run --open \"preview.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false&fiori-tools-rta-mode=true&sap-ui-rta-skip-flex-validation=true#preview-app\"",
    "unit-tests": "fiori run --open 'test/unit/unitTests.qunit.html'",
    "int-tests": "fiori run --open 'test/integration/opaTests.qunit.html'",
    "proxy": "node proxy.js",
    "startall": "npm-run-all --parallel start proxy"
  },
  "ui5": {
    "dependencies": [
      "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling"
    ]
  },
  "sapuxLayer": "CUSTOMER_BASE",
  "dependencies": {
    "cors-anywhere": "^0.4.4"
  }
}

 

To run the app, use start all script

npm run startall

Related Links

https://blog.surges.eu/cors-proxy-server-for-remote-odata-service-in-local-sapui5-dev/
https://www.npmjs.com/package/cors-anywhere

I wonder, why SAP insist on oData. While we can create very lean solutions with rest. 

Hope this helps.

Regards

Hi @AnujaS,

you could achieve this using scheduled autoflow I think. With this you can update fields. Would look something like this:

Simon

entry.title: Can I use BOP with a criteria to only run if my stock is over 10% of the requirement
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 


I would like to run BOP with a criteria that it's only run if my actual stock is above 10% of the requirements in all orders. Anyone know if this is possible? 
Example: 

Order 1 : uncofirmed: 100 PC of Material A 

 

new stock: 103 PC of Material A are available 

BOP Should not run as the stock is not over 10% of the requirement. 

new stock: 7PC: --> Current stock: 110PC 

BOP now runs as the stock is over 10% of requirement 

Order1: Confirmed with 100 PC 

 

I would highly appreciate any responses! Thanks!
Mae

entry.title: SAP Business ByDesign – What’s New 24.05
entry.content:
SAP Business ByDesign 24.05 is now ready!

SAP Business ByDesign 24.05  is now available and the SAP Business ByDesign What’s New blog gives you an overview about the most important new features of SAP Business ByDesign 24.05. These are the so called Focus Topics ().
For these Focus Topics, additional in-depth blogs and videos might be available - just click the link next to the Focus Topic in the What’s New blog.

To get an overview about all new features of SAP Business ByDesign in 24.05 please go to the What's New document.

Cross Topics

    •  
      New Starter Package for Wholesale Industry
      To speed-up the implementation the Wholesale industry Package for SAP Business ByDesign is now available (blog coming soon).

    • Searching for KBAs via KBA number
      Users now can quickly look up KBAs using their KBA ID in addition to text searches in Help Center or Enterprise Search.

    • Additional tiles to Home work center
      Users can now see the additional support related tiles under the Services and Support section for easier access to key information..

For detailed information about What’s New in the Cross Topics area, please click here.

Customer Relationship 
Management


 

 

    •  
      PSM enablement of Action and parameters in Customer Return
      Partners have now an option to create a solution for assigning a service order ID to the Customer Return through PDI solution.

    • PSM Enablement of the Action “Block” of planning area of Material
      Partners have now an option to block planning area via PDI solution or webservice or ODATA using "Block” action of planning area in the material master.
      .
For detailed information about What’s New in the Customer Relationship Management area, please click here.

Localization



 



    • Mexico
      New tax events for International Air TransportationEntity Identifier (LEI) – Mass Data Maintenance
      Supplier invoices and customer invoices can now be created with 2% and 4% tax rates and all such transactions will now be reported in the 'Periodic VAT Returns' and 'A-29 Report' tax returns for Mexico.

For detailed information about all country/region-specific features, please click here.

Solution Extensions



 


    • NobleQ – Sales Commission
      Beneficiaries can be rewarded based on their sales performance, thus allowing you to recognize talent, reward achievements and conflict management.

    • all4cloud – procure4cloud
      Integration of SAP Business ByDesign with SAP Ariba enabling automatice transfer of orders generated in SAP Ariba to SAP Business ByDesign and back incl. order confirmations and invoices.

    • Stretch AB– Localization Sweden
      The localization solution for Sweden supports local requirements such as tax report, payment file, bank statement, EC sales list, SIE, Supplier invoice accrual and automatic exchange rate update (SAP store).

    • Snap Int – SAF-T: Report Solution for Romania
      Ensure tax compliance with SAF-T Reporting Solution, tailor-made for Romania's tax system. Whether you are a local entity or a VAT-registered non-resident company, Snap Int SAF-T solution seamlessly integrates with SAP Business ByDesign, providing a comprehensive tool for the new tax reporting standards mandated from January 2022.
      .

The Partner innovation roadmap information is regularly update in this blog.

entry.title: Re: In service cloud v2, how can we auto-escalate the cas...
entry.content:
Hi @Simon1982,

Where can I find scheduled autoflow in service cloud v2. Right now I can see only autoflow in the tenant where there is only option to send notification and mail. There is no field update option.

Thanks,

Anuja

entry.title: Re: UI5 Build Error when deploying to SAPUI5 ABAP Repo
entry.content:
Repository npm.sap.com has retired in Feb 2022. See note: https://me.sap.com/notes/3109201

If you have .npmrc file for your project, make sure to remove any reference to this repository.  It might be that you have an old package-lock.json file holding this old value. In this case, you can try to delete it and re-build.

entry.title: SAP AI Launchpad - Deployment is in pending status for a day with No errors in logs
entry.content: Deployments in SAP AI Launchpad struck in Pending status, same docker image when deployed in local it works fine. Not seeing any errors in logs difficult to debug it.

entry.title: Re: HTTP Status 404 - not found error into integration fr...
entry.content:
Hi nikunjmehta2290

If it was working fine before and suddenly you faced this issue then please check if port 8080 and 8443 are blocked ?

Try to access B1if using using below URLs from Integration server browser:

 https://b1if server address:8443  

http://b1if server address:8080

If issue is with fresh installation, kindly perform clean reinstall with below steps:

1. Uninstall the Integration Component from control panel
2. Clean up the folder %temp%
3. Delete the folder, where the integration component were previously installed, if it exists
4. Delete B1if / IFSERV database schema
5. Remove antivirus and firewall protection
6. Remove the Java from add-removeprograms section, if exists
7. Delete the hidden folder named "Zero G Registry" in "C:\Program Files (x86)\".
If you do notsee this folder, change the settings in Windows Explorer to show hidden files/folders
8. Open Windows Registry and delete thekey folders below:
----
32-bit server: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP\Business One Integration
32-bit server:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\Tomcatx
----
64-bit server:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\SAP\Business One Integration
64-bit server:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\Tomcat6
System variables list.
10. From SQLServer Management Studio, go to SBOCOMMON database and delete/truncate all records from these 3 tables: SLSP, SLSPP, and SEVT.
11. Reboot the computer
12. Install the Integration component again
13. Restart the Integration service.

If still having issue, please create incident using component SBO-INT-B1IF.

Thank You.
Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: What is the function of "Show All Objects" within the GRC ARA user level risk analysis?
entry.content:
Under the section Report Options within the User Level risk analysis it is possible to select Show All Objects.

However when running the analysis with or without the Show All Objects selected, the outcome at my side seems the same.

Can anyone explain what this function of Show All Objects should do?

Hi,

Check Point 3.7 in the SAP Note 1618978 for the error.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Unable to add Reference object
entry.content:
Hi Suresh,

it seems you don't use an S/4HANA Cloud system as described in the tutorial.
I_SALESORDERTP view is not available in Steampunk systems.
Best regards,

Peter

entry.title: Re: TOTAL CONSUMPTION VS SALES QUERY
entry.content:
Hi Brad,

The way you are joining the tables will limit your data.  Say there are invoices on days with no production orders, those will be lost.

I used a CTE table setup here:

-- DECLARE VARIABLES

DECLARE @Date1 DATETIME
DECLARE @Date2 DATETIME
 
SET @Date1 = (SELECT MIN(S0.[DocDate]) FROM OINM S0 WHERE S0.[DocDate] >= '[%0]');
SET @Date2 = (SELECT MAX(S1.[DocDate]) FROM OINM S1 WHERE S1.[DocDate] <= '[%1]');

-- DEFINE TABLE

WITH ConsumptionData AS
(		
SELECT 
	 D0.[ItemCode]
	,D0.[DocDate] AS 'Date'
	,D0.[Quantity] AS 'Sold'
	,0 AS 'Issued'
	,D0.[Quantity] AS 'Combined'
FROM
	INV1 D0 WITH (NOLOCK)
	INNER JOIN OINV D1 WITH (NOLOCK) ON D0.[DocEntry] = D1.[DocEntry]
WHERE D1.[CANCELED] = 'N'

UNION ALL

SELECT 
	 D0.[ItemCode]
	,D0.[DocDate] AS 'Date'
	,D0.[Quantity] * -1 AS 'Sold'
	,0 AS 'Issued'
	,D0.[Quantity] * -1 AS 'Combined'
FROM
	RIN1 D0 WITH (NOLOCK)
	INNER JOIN ORIN D1 WITH (NOLOCK) ON D0.[DocEntry] = D1.[DocEntry]
WHERE D1.[CANCELED] = 'N'

UNION ALL

SELECT
	 D0.[ItemCode]
	,D1.[PostDate] AS 'Date'
	,0 AS 'Sold'
	,D0.[IssuedQty] AS 'Issued'
	,D0.[IssuedQty] AS 'Combined'
FROM
	WOR1 D0 WITH (NOLOCK)
	INNER JOIN OWOR D1 WITH (NOLOCK) ON D0.[DocEntry] = D1.[DocEntry] 
WHERE D1.[Status] IN ('R', 'L')
)

-- PULL DATA

SELECT
	 T0.[ItemCode]
	,T0.[ItemName]
	,ISNULL(SUM(X.[Issued]),0) AS 'Consumed In Production'
	,ISNULL(SUM(X.[Sold]),0) AS 'Sold Quantity'
	,ISNULL(SUM(X.[Combined]),0) AS 'Total Consumption'

FROM
	OITM T0 WITH (NOLOCK)
	LEFT JOIN ConsumptionData X ON T0.[ItemCode] = X.[ItemCode]

WHERE X.[Date] BETWEEN @Date1 AND @Date2

GROUP BY T0.[ItemCode], T0.[ItemName]

ORDER BY T0.[ItemCode]

You can also use a derived table:

-- DECLARE VARIABLES

DECLARE @Date1 DATETIME
DECLARE @Date2 DATETIME
 
SET @Date1 = (SELECT MIN(S0.[DocDate]) FROM OINM S0 WHERE S0.[DocDate] >= '[%0]')
SET @Date2 = (SELECT MAX(S1.[DocDate]) FROM OINM S1 WHERE S1.[DocDate] <= '[%1]')

-- PULL DATA

SELECT
	 T0.[ItemCode]
	,T0.[ItemName]
	,ISNULL(SUM(X.[Issued]),0) AS 'Consumed In Production'
	,ISNULL(SUM(X.[Sold]),0) AS 'Sold Quantity'
	,ISNULL(SUM(X.[Combined]),0) AS 'Total Consumption'

FROM
	OITM T0 WITH (NOLOCK)
	LEFT JOIN (
		SELECT 
			 D0.[ItemCode]
			,D0.[DocDate] AS 'Date'
			,D0.[Quantity] AS 'Sold'
			,0 AS 'Issued'
			,D0.[Quantity] AS 'Combined'
		FROM
			INV1 D0 WITH (NOLOCK)
			INNER JOIN OINV D1 WITH (NOLOCK) ON D0.[DocEntry] = D1.[DocEntry]
		WHERE D1.[CANCELED] = 'N'

		UNION ALL

		SELECT 
			 D0.[ItemCode]
			,D0.[DocDate] AS 'Date'
			,D0.[Quantity] * -1 AS 'Sold'
			,0 AS 'Issued'
			,D0.[Quantity] * -1 AS 'Combined'
		FROM
			RIN1 D0 WITH (NOLOCK)
			INNER JOIN ORIN D1 WITH (NOLOCK) ON D0.[DocEntry] = D1.[DocEntry]
		WHERE D1.[CANCELED] = 'N'

		UNION ALL

		SELECT
			 D0.[ItemCode]
			,D1.[PostDate] AS 'Date'
			,0 AS 'Sold'
			,D0.[IssuedQty] AS 'Issued'
			,D0.[IssuedQty] AS 'Combined'
		FROM
			WOR1 D0 WITH (NOLOCK)
			INNER JOIN OWOR D1 WITH (NOLOCK) ON D0.[DocEntry] = D1.[DocEntry] 
		WHERE D1.[Status] IN ('R', 'L')
	) X ON T0.[ItemCode] = X.[ItemCode]

WHERE X.[Date] BETWEEN @Date1 AND @Date2

GROUP BY T0.[ItemCode], T0.[ItemName]

ORDER BY T0.[ItemCode]

Both are roughly the same but slightly different structures.  I just stacked the three sets of rows and then group them at the end.

I hope you can follow what I'm doing.  I also added in credit notes.  But you can remove that part if you don't want it there.

I tested these and they seem to do what you want, you could further filter the item list.  The way I did it you would still get zero quantity items to give you a full list even if something wasn't consumed.  But you could just INNER join the data table or add a filter as needed.

Let me know if this does what you need,

Mike

entry.title: Job Profile Builder - Assign more than one Job Profile to Employee
entry.content:
Dear Community,

 

I got the request to map several (or more than one) Job Profile to employee. As often employees are taking over more than one job at the same time.

Do you have an idea if this is somehow feasible? (Without EC)

 

Many thanks in advance.

 

Best regards,

Gunnar

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration with SuccessFactors Through CPI: A Step-by-Step Handbook for Seamless Connectivi
entry.content: Great sharing @nimra_younus

entry.title: Add Horizontal Scrolling to Post Group Journal Entries UI
entry.content:
Dear community,

Our Group Reporting customer actively uses many custom-specific dimensions, totaling more than 20 dimensions that need to be filled in the Post Group Journal Entries app. The user can set up and save the column structure of the line items UI part. However, if the number of columns exceeds the monitor's width, every additional column is displayed in a new line. This results in the user seeing the first 12 columns in the first line and the next 8 columns in subsequent lines, leading to 9 lines for one data row.

While it is possible to adjust the browser zoom or to navigate to the detailed UI for one data row (on the right side), this is not the preferred solution. The customer desires that a single data row remains presented in one line regardless of the number of displayed columns, with navigation enabled by horizontal scrolling.

Is there any possibility to make the UI horizontally scrollable?

Kind regards,

Dmitrij

entry.title: Re: Unable to add Reference object
entry.content: Hi peterpersiel, we do have customizing and development tenant, can please point to the documentation. i did try find it in finding but the above link was closest i could find. it would help us a lot if can point us to the documentation. Thanks

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - IP Address Changes on AWS
entry.content:
Overview

Due to sporadic network connection resets on Amazon Web Services (AWS), all supported AWS regions will get new inbound network load balancers with adjusted settings. This will change the server IP addresses of all SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk") instances in these AWS regions. No change on customer side is required with solely DNS based name resolution. If plain IP addresses are used in network devices like firewalls to control traffic to SAP BTP ABAP Environment instances, the IP addresses have to be adjusted depending on the region of the used SAP BTP ABAP Environment instances as follows. This change may also affect on-premise integration scenarios with SAP Cloud Connector, since this component opens a network connection to the inbound network of a SAP BTP ABAP Environment instance.

 

IP Address Changes on AWS

ap10 - Australia (Sydney):
3.106.174.169
54.153.216.203

ap11 - Singapore:
54.254.57.62
52.74.178.84

ap12 - South Korea (Seoul):
43.203.57.204
43.200.64.19

br10 - Brazil (São Paulo):
54.232.231.172
54.94.227.67

ca10 - Canada (Montreal):
15.156.15.80
15.157.88.217

eu10 - Europe (Frankfurt):
18.199.188.98
18.158.34.94
18.185.7.213

eu11 - Europe (Frankfurt) EU Access:
3.126.131.68
52.58.68.219
3.77.134.139

jp10 - Japan (Tokyo):
52.199.132.111
54.249.69.156

us10 - US East (VA):
44.199.118.107
3.221.108.94
44.193.178.208

The listed IP addresses will become valid on May 27, 2024. Previously used IP addresses will become invalid on June 10, 2024.

entry.title: Re: Setup VSCcode for SAP Commerce
entry.content:
Instead of VS Code, use IntelliJ Community IDE with SAP-Commerce-Developers-Toolset plug-in > https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/12867-sap-commerce-developers-toolset

This combination works. No need to make your life difficult.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Extraction Filter for M2S JCDS
entry.content:
Hi Niels,

Please try: SUBSTRING(OBJNR,1,2) = 'OR'

Documentation on this function can be found here: String Functions and Operators — Presto 0.284 Documentation (prestodb.io)

Hope this helps,

Evan

entry.title: SAP CodeJam in Lisbon
entry.content:
Hello Thomas,

we would like to host a SAP CodeJam in Lisbon, Portugal.

With the possibility of having the following topics would be great:

  • Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python
  • With SAP Build, Create Apps, Processes, and Business Sites Without Coding

Regarding the dates we are flexible, but we want to avoid the vacation period between July and August.

We can host up to 40 participants and would organize lunch and snacks. Start would be at 9 am, end would be around 5 pm.

Thanks, and kind regards

Jaime

entry.title: Re: Generate PFX file to connect SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Mahesh, 

found your article while searching if there was a better way to do this, and wanted to share that you can reformat the cert and key in one go in NotePad++ if you use this

Basically do Regular expression and replace \\n (first \ escapes the 2nd one so it's not interpreted) by \n (actually interpreted as a new line).

For people not comfortable with OpenSSL command line, you can as well use a GUI tool like KeyStore explorer (free and OpenSource) to build the PFX file from the Certificate and Key files. KeyStore Explorer (keystore-explorer.org)

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi @CarsonWongIVC yes the assessements cover the delta of how the product/solution you are certified in evolved over the last releases. There is usually content to prepare for the assessments offered in addition or a live session. The assessment pass rate is 80% and you have unlimited trials.

Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: Re: Goal Visibility - make private goals public
entry.content:
Hi EstherM,

You can grant roles with private-access permission, so users with granted permission can view the private goals.
The share permission can allow user to make a goal public or private.

Following is the Guide for attributes related to the visibility field:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PERFORMANCE_AND_GOALS/4a24e48b5c384b18a81f6b661305021e/c9776e117b42403d8d6021d26adcc506.html

Following is the Guide for action permission settings, you can find the related attributes in the table:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PERFORMANCE_AND_GOALS/4a24e48b5c384b18a81f6b661305021e/012780c25b9b455fb8e2dc223ab93a3c.html#overview

BR,
Yilin

entry.title: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP BTP - Blog 7 Interview
entry.content:
Blog 7: The Role of Automation for Transformation - The background Interview

 

My colleague Philipp Zaltenbach posted an inspiring blog last week and we had a background interview for more information.

Timothy Kaufmann: Philipp, why are you so enthusiastic about Automation with SAP?

Philipp Zaltenbach:There are especially two aspects that I want to highlight. First is versatility. I really like that automation technologies can be applied to such a broad range of scenarios. We have customers that use our technologies for simple approval workflows. These may differ from one Line-of-Business to the other. On the other hand, we have customers such as Lufthansa Technik who use it for complex automation scenarios that touch a lot of applications, both SAP and Non-SAP. The second point is ease-of-use. Many customers who experience our offering in a hands-on workshop are impressed at how quick they can build something without having had extensive training beforehand.

 

Timothy Kaufmann: Where do you see the USP for SAP?

Philipp Zaltenbach: In my view there are three points that I would stress. First, we have a unified end-2-end offering that can be applied in a modular fashion. This is really unique, given the range of capabilities overall. Second, we have built a lot of pre-built content, such as integration packages, workflows or RPA bots, that can serve as a starting point and avoid reinventing the wheel. Third, the tight and robust integration into our SAP application landscape, be it an S/4HANA system, or an LoB application such as SuccessFactors.

 

Timothy Kaufmann: How do you see the role of Automation for Clean Core?

Philipp Zaltenbach: As I have shared in my blog article, our Enterprise Automation offering can play an important role in Clean Core initiatives and related ERP modernization projects. It can help both before the move – to analyze, clean-up and streamline processes – but also after the move, to help with continuous process optimization.

 

Timothy Kaufmann: How do you see the potential of AI for Automation?

Philipp Zaltenbach: With the advent of GenAI we see a massive opportunity for automation. Many customers still have scenarios that require working with unstructured documents such as PDF files. Here our embedded AI services can provide drastic benefits for automated document extraction and processing. On the other hand, we also strive to make the development process even more accessible and simpler by using GenAI based assistance. Users will then be able to talk to the system in natural language and with that they can create entire process automations from scratch.

 

Timothy Kaufmann: Where do you see the difference between Workflow and Automation?

Automation is a very large area that covers many topics, for example integration, RPA-tools or workflow engines just to name a few. Hence, workflow can be considered as a sub-topic of automation. So workflow can be part of an overall automation that is made of different building blocks, comprising e.g. workflow elements (such as approval forms and business rules), as well as RPA elements (such as bots for UI automation of an application).

 

Timothy Kaufmann: When you talk to companies about Automation, where do you see the biggest challenges?

Philipp Zaltenbach: The challenges vary from customer to customer. Some customers struggle with where to start and which use cases to tackle. For that we can offer workshops with SAP Consulting or partners to identify the most promising areas and scenarios. Also customers often look for how to get familiar with our products. Here, we have self-guided, interactive learning journeys (e.g. the one for SAP Build) as well as hands-on workshops as a starting point.

If you want to read all blogs:

  • Blog 1: The Central Role of Clean Core and AI: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Bu... - SAP Community
  • Blog 2: The Role of BTP as Extension and Innovation Platform: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Bu... - SAP Community
  • Blog 3: How SAP Clean Core Strategy can accelerate your Transformation SAP Clean Core Strategy: The Key to Business Transformation
  • Blog 4: Mastering the Data Dance: The Potential of Clean Core and AI 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Bu... - SAP Community
  • Blog 5: How hybrid Integration upholds the role of Clean Core: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Bu... - SAP Community
  • Blog 6: Clean Core Extensibility 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Bu... - SAP Community
  • Blog 7: The Role of Automation for transformation: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Bu... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
@Attila I have the same issue. After a successfull installation of the 2022 version on Docker it works for a while, and then after a couple of hours or the next day it stops working and I get the 'Connection to partner 127..... broken". So after tryin a few things in vain, I deleted the container, installed it again, it worked again, and now on the next day I get the same error again and can no longer open my Docker instance in SAP GUI. I'm not doing anything differently compared to installing and running the 1909 version and never had this issue before.

Anybody found a solution yet? So far the 2022 version is unusable.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite : Cloud Integration - User Propagation in Transport
entry.content:
Hi @deepakgd , 

Is there any planned updates to feature where the check box for "Propagate logged-in user as transport owner" ticked on and developer not able to untick it? 

As this will helps tremendously when looking at CTM side and there is good readability as to which user created this transport? 

Will reduce problem from an audit perspective as well.

entry.title: Re: Reporting on all Performance Form Scores
entry.content:
Hi Jarel,

The item rating is "Competency Official Rating".
"Competency Other Rating" will pull other users' unofficial rating.
If self rating box is enabled, you can find employee self rating with "Competency Self Rating".

BR,
Yilin

entry.title: Re: Release Value not updated in Value Contract when sale...
entry.content:
Hi Lydia, 

you could try changing the completion rule (VBAP-ERLRE) in customizing (Tx. VOV7) to E. Then make a test. However the existing documents can not be updated.

Br,

Sreemohan

entry.title: Re: Is there an SDK installation for sap 10.00.240?
entry.content:
Hi odele,

Are you using SAPBusinessOneSDK.dll from SP2402 version only?

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: In service cloud v2, how can we auto-escalate the cas...

entry.title: Not able to read MTA - user provided variables from code
entry.content:
Not able to read MTA - user provided variables from code

the user variable shows up in btp cockpit for the application

I am able to see other variables in process.env when in debug

Also added below in node module.

let envinfo = require('@sap/xsenv').loadEnv()

any pointers on how to achieve this or what is wrong ?

entry.title: Re: Identity Access Management (IAM) Reference Architectures 2024
entry.content:
Hello,

what is the reference recommendation how to structure the SAP Cloud Identity Service across stages?

So usually there are at least one productive and one nonProductive instance of SAP Cloud Identity Service (simply called IAS-PROD and IAS-TEST). Should the IAS-PROD be used for any or only for productive applications? 

Recommendations under https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/tenant-types are not clear here in my opinion:

  • "Test tenants are provided where they can test new features before implementing them productively"
    • This indicates that IAS-TEST should not be used for authentication against DEV or TEST application because testing new feature might break the authentication flow
  • "The test tenant is also the default identity provider for the test landscape"
    • This indicates that IAS-TEST should be used for authentication against nonProdutive applications. But how about a three-tier backend landscape. Should a IAS-DEV be ordered?
  • "for some test instances the trust can only be configured to Identity Authentication test tenants"
    • so if we like to use IAS-PROD for each application (except Sandboxes) we have some exceptionell applications which can't be linked directly with IAS-PROD?

"This reference architecture expects the user present in IdDS": In case IAS-PROD is used for each application, this means that also test account should be created here.

Any considerations to use a productive Corporate IdP with nonProductive IAS?

entry.title: Re: New in Production Accounting – Event-Based Production Cost Posting
entry.content: Hi, can you post the link to the video. I cannot open it.

entry.title: Re: Quick Customer Search SCCO
entry.content:
Hi Sebastián

Maybe because there are no "default sort columns" defined in your customer search dialog settings. If not set anything there, I would assume, that the records are ordered by sequence of table entries written into the database. 

Kind Regards

Klaus

entry.title: E-way bill generate time error , SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am facing below 2 different type error while generate the AR Invoice including E-Way bill details. Here, No API is connected with SAP B1. 

Error 1 : Cannot call the electronic file manager. Contact technical support. EDS URI not defined - Check SLD

Error 2 : Internal Error. Details can be found in System Messages Log in 'Context'. Missing or invalid mapping - 0. Try again or contact technical support.

SAP Business One SQL Version

Regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - SAP AI Services

entry.title: Re: unable to trigger EWM Goods Receipt Using Packing POD

entry.title: Re: Story will not load
entry.content: You absolute genius! That has saved me so much work.

entry.title: Re: Story will not load
entry.content: Do you happen to know why this happened for you? I don't recall making any changes to that specific page and can't get into it to try and troubleshoot.

entry.title: Re: Authorization in BOPF generated through CDS
entry.content:
Hi Seph,

You can only maintain the authorization class for generated business objects.

The details are mentioned in the help documentation : https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/cc0c305d2fab47bd808adcad3ca7ee9d/11682c24ecca4ce4a7939371cb6bb3d6.html?locale=en-US

Thanks and Regards,

Shyam

entry.title: Re: Reverse Document Posted in QA11
entry.content:
Hi Antonio,

According the SAP documentation (and my tests) the program indeed reverses all the material documents generated by the UD so only an individual reversal of a material document is not possible.

You could mitigate by making a material booking with using movement types 322/349 so the material is back into quality stock again, although the backdraft is that you miss the opportunity to set another UD with a new material document related to the inspection lot (but at least the material is back into Quality stock).

Herman

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David_Chaviano,
I'm from Vietnam.
I'm an SAP MM Functional Consutant (ECC).
I want to upskill myself in SAP technology and to study SAP rigorously and get a new job opportunity.
I hope you will consider my application for the free certification in SAP S4 HANA.
Thanks a Lot!
Regards,

entry.title: May Developer Challenge - SAP AI Services - Week 3
entry.content:
Welcome to week 3 of the AI Developer Challenge! I am so excited about every single submission! Now I don’t know about you but in my family, we love avocado! Especially my youngest could eat it all the time. Let’s use the Data Attribute Recommendation service to build an avocado price prediction engine! With Data Attribute Recommendation you can train regression as well as classification models and you can use the SAP AI Launchpad, Postman, Swagger or the AI API Python SDK to implement your use case! With Data Attribute Recommendation you need to specify a model template or a business blueprint to specify what kind of algorithm should be used.

For this challenge we will use the regression model template to implement an avocado price predictor! If you do not like avocado, feel free to come up with another use case and search for a fitting dataset (e.g. here).

This week we start by uploading and preparing our dataset schema and our dataset! For that we will be using the AI API which is a standardized way to interact with different AI runtimes and services such as SAP AI Core or Data Attribute Recommendation.

  1. Go to your BTP trial account and create a DAR instance (you can use the booster): https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-aibus-dar-service-instance.html
  2. Download this dataset
  3. Download the postman collection for the Data Attribute Recommendation service and import the folder to Postman
  4. Go to the environment tab on the left and add the information from your DAR service key from step 1.
    1. authUrl = url from the uaa section in your .json file
    2. url = url
  5. Make sure to select the correct environment and go back to the Collections tab to create a token with the Get XSUAA OAuth Token GET request in the Authorization folder. If everything is set up correctly it will take the url, username and password from your environmental variables you configured in step 4. It will also assign the token to the correct variable in the environment for further use. Make sure to repeat this step once the token timed out.
  6. Create a dataset_schema.json file
  7. Go back to the Environments tab and assign a folder name (e.g. avocado_price_predictor) and a datasetSchemaFileRegression name (e.g. dataset_schema.json) as well as a datasetFileRegression name (e.g. avocado_data.csv)
  8. Upload a dataset schema with the PUT Upload Dataset Schema Regression request and make sure to upload your dataset_schema.json
  9. Upload this dataset with the PUT Upload Dataset Regression request
  10. Create a dataset schema artifact with the POST Create Dataset Schema Artifact Regression request (the required values were assigned automatically to your environment)
  11. Create a dataset artifact with the POST Create Dataset Artifact Regression request (the required values were assigned automatically to your environment)
  12. Learn more about AI API and the underlying concepts such as artifacts
  13. Submission is again a screenshot of the last POST request and the correct returned result:
  14. Stay tuned for next week to train your model!

entry.title: Re: Advanced Financial Closing: Change communication system
entry.content: Thank you Thomas for your answer.

entry.title: In service cloud v2, case fields are not getting updated using patch API
entry.content: I am using https://api.sap.com/api/SalesSvcCloudV2_case/tryout PATCH api to update the field in existing case in Service Cloud v2. I am facing 412 error when I hit the patch request. I have attached screenshot below.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
@Attila Let us state it this way: maybe unusable with your network settings or other setup you are using at the moment. I installed it on a debian12 PC like a week ago and is still running smoothly.

I just made sure that the host PC always has the same IP address and doesn't get a new one on each restart for example.

entry.title: Enhanced Initial Data Migration Profile in SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the enhancement of the “Initial Data Migration Profile” in SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling.

Figure 1: The old and new view of the Initial Data Migration Profile

The Initial Data Migration Profile of the SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling analyzes important SAP ERP 6.0 data objects and their data volumes. The profile data delivers valuable input for the often tedious and costly exercise of manually scoping and assessing data objects. With this newest release, we have enhanced the data collector and revamped the user experience when reviewing the results, displaying more information, and offering additional capabilities for slicing and dicing the information.

The following list highlights the new and enhanced features that are described in more detail further below:

  • Where possible, a deeper level of data analysis providing more insight into the business objects
  • Filtering and grouping functions to enhance the user experience
  • Introduction of new interactive UI elements to assist in the visualization of the findings
  • The ability to run “what-if” scenarios supporting a selective data transition approach
  • Where applicable, insight into the history of the data volume
  • Identification of unused data objects and deprecated organizational units

The New Data Collector

We completely redesigned the Initial Data Profile data collector from scratch, replacing the existing collector as it only provided limited information regarding the analyzed data objects.

The scoping and development of the new data collector was done in cooperation and collaboration with the SAP Business Data Transformation Solutions team. SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling and SAP Business Transformation Center now use the same data collector. This alignment helps expedite the move from evaluating and scoping to planning and conducting a selective data transition project.

Business objects are now used as data object identifiers which are more commonly used to represent real-world artifacts than database table names. In total 215 business objects are now analyzed by the data collector.

For many business objects, we have extended the analysis beyond the high-level data volume figures and now provide insights into additional valuable data. For instance, you can now group results by organizational elements (e.g. company codes, plants, or sales organizations), depending on the attributes of the business object.

For business objects containing the necessary date attributes, the data volumes can also be categorized in a historical context by defining year range filters.

Additionally, the new data collector now determines the first and last recorded activity dates for applicable business objects, helping to identify business objects no longer in use and resulting in a leaner data scope for the data migration.

These added values help project teams decide on the best transition approach. For instance, the result of the Initial Data Migration Profile can be used to evaluate the feasibility of a selective data transition approach by gaining initial insight into expected data volumes and facilitating the definition of the data scope. In case the selective data transition approach is an option for your transition project, you might then continue to investigate the data in detail and create a digital blueprint with the  tool.

The New Initial Data Migration Profile User Experience

Figure 2: The new Initial Data Migration Profile dashboard card

The dashboard card of the Initial Data Migration Profile presents now a pie chart instead of a bar chart indicating the top 6 business objects by volume (fig. 2). To open the detail view, select any item from the pie chart or select the dashboard card header.

The detailed view of the Initial Data Migration Profile was newly created from scratch and follows a flexible column layout model, available in many other SAP Fiori apps. The view allows users to expand the column they want to focus on and switch between different layouts easily.

Figure 3: The new Initial Data Migration Profile view in 1-column mode

By default, the view displays a table of all analyzed business objects (fig. 3) including the following columns:

  • business object name
  • application component
  • related table name of the business object
  • data type (master or transactional data)
  • latest activity
  • total number of items

Initially, the table is sorted by the number of items in descending order which can be changed using the corresponding button on the top right of the table control. The content of the table can also be exported to a spreadsheet.

To quickly search for a specific business object, simply start typing the name of the object into the search field on top of the table and the table will filter accordingly.

Business objects with in-depth data analysis available have a detail indicator to the right of the last column in the row. Selecting a business object switches the view to the 2-column (detail) mode. The business object list is now shown in a compressed format on the left, while the detail data of the selected business object is presented on the right side (fig. 4).

To increase the space of the detail column to full screen or to close the detail column, use the corresponding buttons in the top right corner.

Figure 4: 2-column mode of the view showing the details of the selected business object

The right column within the view is divided into two parts. On the top part, the filter criteria and KPIs of the selected business object are shown. The lower section presents charts and tables to visualize and support analyzing the data based on the selected filter criteria.

The capabilities that can be used to group and filter the business object data depend on the attributes of the selected business object. For example, the business object “FI Documents” has the filter criteria year and company code available. Other business objects, like “Equipment” do not contain any aspect of time but can be grouped by plant. There are also objects that do not have any grouping capability at all, for instance, the business object “Status Management.” For these business objects no detailed data is available.

When the business object data contains date attributes, use the range slider control to set different intervals by year. The data is immediately filtered according to the selected range. To select filter criteria for organizational key elements, like company code or plant, use the corresponding multi-select combo boxes. All selected filter criteria are applied to the data immediately using “AND” logic.

The Selected Data Ratio KPI visualizes the ratio between the total data volume of the selected business object and the data volume based on the applied filter conditions. Additionally, the oldest and the latest activity dates of the business object are shown in the KPI section. The values for the total data volume and the filtered data volume are displayed helping to identify unused or no longer used business objects.

Depending on the capabilities of the business object’s grouping criteria, you can select different options to present and examine the business object volume data by using the toolbar on top of the data section. For example, the data within the “FI Document” business object can be analyzed using the following charts and tables:

Figure 5: Different options to present and examine the business object data

As previoulsy, all data in the tables can be exported to a spreadsheet for further processing by selecting the related button on top of the table.

Filter Criteria Views

To increase usability, the new Initial Data Migration Profile allows users not only to define and activate filter criteria for organizational and date-related key figures (e.g., company code, plants, year of creation, etc.) but to store them persistently in filter criteria views, which – once activated – are applied to all business object data, provided the criteria fields exist in the business object (otherwise the filter criteria is ignored).

This feature supports  discussions about the data migration scope: You can define filter criteria views for different data migration scenarios and use them for what-if analysis, e. g. to evaluate the impact of migrating 3 or 4 years of historic data to the new system or to analyze the effects of carving out one or more company codes.

To set up a filter criteria view, expand the filter section on top of the business object table by selecting the related button (see fig. 6). The filter section shows multi-select combo boxes for all available filter criteria, in which you can define the criteria values for your filter criteria view. To apply the filter criteria view, use the “Go” button below the filter section.

Figure 6: Setting up filter criteria views which are applied to all business objects

If you want to save your filter criteria view persistently for the analysis, select the drop-down button on top of the filter section(next to the caption “Standard”; see fig. 6). This opens the “My View” pop-up window, which lists already existing filter criteria views of the analysis and offers buttons to either save the current selected filter criteria into a new filter criteria view or to manage existing ones (see fig. 7).

Figure 7: Create and manage filter criteria views

To run an SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling analysis, follow the instructions described in SAP Note 3112362.

You can also update an already existing analysis: Implement the latest version of SAP Note 3112362 and run the analysis in your SAP ERP system again. Then use the “Update Analysis“ feature of the SAP Readiness Check app to update the analysis data.

As always, we are interested in your feedback!

Your SAP Readiness Check team

entry.title: Re: Advanced Financial Closing: Change communication system
entry.content: We transfered more or less all steps from our excel, therefore we used many notes to also include non-SAP tasks to fully use the AFC as a reporting and controlling tool. Therefore I think the task lists models can also not be used.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

there is no concrete schedule available to provide an integrated and seamless advanced intercompany stock transfer process in a 2-tier or multisystem environment.

Generally I assume that the design of an advanced ICo stock transfer process in a multisystem environment will differ significantly from the current process design of adv ICo stock transfer within one system. Main reason is that in the process within one system the ICo purchase order has direct access to the delivering plant and is the main reference for the outbound delivery.

A multisystem process is based on a sell-from-stock (with optional VSIT) process in one system and a procure to stock (with optional VSIT) process in the other system.
Both processes are already available now. Combining both processes leads to an ICo stock transfer process in a multisystem environment that is non-integrated and non-seamless.

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: Re: Story will not load
entry.content: I don't know why it happened. We had last changes in the story last week but the error appeared yesterday the first time. Maybe a bugfix which came to our tenant causes the problem. We are currently on version 2024.2.25 (client) / 2024.2.21 (server).

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI latest patch
entry.content: Well, this blog exists and is being updated regularly for 10 years now. Thank you for your kudos/likes and visits! ❤️

entry.title: audio files as assets in app
entry.content:
Hello,

@Dan_Wroblewski , @MaiaraEllwanger , @marihirvi

for some reasons of internet access, I need the app to be completely autonomous and not to have to load audio files from external urls. I see that images can be loaded autonomously as image assets during the programming of the app in SAP Build Apps. I ask to have the same function for audio files (I dont need for video files) to load them as assets inside the apps. How can this be achieved or a new component need to be designed? Hope this can be solved asap, thank you!

Best regards

Pierre-Yves

entry.title: Re: Is there an SDK installation for sap 10.00.240?
entry.content:
Hi,

In this addon I have used 230 but now I need to make adjustments for the new tax law in Israel.

That is why I need access to the allocation number property.

Kind regards,

Odele

entry.title: Re: Hii Expert,I am having issue related to retention mon...
entry.content: Hello Sanan, First of all thanking you for your valuable response, But updating at directly table level is not a permanent solution as my client belongs to coal psu. So they have frequent and huge issues in N number of purchase order. That's why I am searching for bapi, function module or any other process. So that we can propose for customization and if any standard process are there then please let me know.
